phase3_findings.description: issues:read comment claims the #312 endpoint-permission lint requires the issues family be declared; the label endpoints are not among the lint's six recognized families, so the lint does not require it. Documented falsehood (calibrated up from Suggestion via the receiving-code-review documented-falsehood carve-out; concrete failing input: removing issues:read keeps the lint at 0 violations vs pull-requests removal -> 1).
EXTRAS compose var is a second hand-copied mirror of .devflow/config.json devflow_implement.allowed_tools with no sync pin (IMPLEMENT is pinned, EXTRAS is not).
run.sh #450 pin comment 'byte-token identity' / 'byte-synced' overstates a token-level (whitespace-stripped) comparison; self-corrected by the next sentence.

implement-probe job-intro states the implement-tier anchor denial as settled fact ('so the helpers never run') while the changeset/Shape-1 hedge 'suspected'; the tier the probe measures is exactly what is unproven. Not locally verifiable (matcher semantics only provable in cloud).

implement-probe verdict python (REFORMULATED/discriminator classifier) has no local unit test; consistent with the sibling probe precedent (cloud-only measurement), though newer probes extract verdict logic into tested helpers.

DENIED verdict keys on the marker substring without the shape discriminator, so a denied reformulation of shape 1 attributes to the unexpanded-anchor row; inherited assumption shared with the sibling probe job, fails toward UNATTEMPTED not false PERMITTED.

A valid-but-degenerate parse (json.loads succeeds returning a scalar/no tool_use) yields all-UNATTEMPTED with no WARNING; fails toward UNATTEMPTED (safe), mitigated by the 'Raw denial entries (0)' line.

fix_decisions.claim_text: EXTRAS is an unpinned coupled mirror of config allowed_tools
fix_decisions.evidence: Real but low-impact: repo-internal, human-dispatched measurement job; no shipped consumer depends on EXTRAS. Author deliberately pinned IMPLEMENT (the real profile copy) not EXTRAS (config extras). Already logged as a candidate future pin in the prior run's shadow record. Below the important fix threshold.

fix_decisions.claim_text: 'byte-token identity' phrasing overstates the token-level comparison
fix_decisions.evidence: Suggestion; the next sentence in the same comment describes the behavior accurately. Cosmetic phrasing nit, no behavioral impact.

fix_decisions.claim_text: job-intro states implement-tier anchor denial as fact vs 'suspected' elsewhere
fix_decisions.evidence: Suggestion, comment epistemic-posture nit. The claim is the well-motivated hypothesis grounded in #401 (review-tier fact); it is not locally verifiable (matcher semantics only provable in the real cloud action per MEMORY) so it does not meet the documented-falsehood carve-out (no concrete failing input). Not chased on an otherwise non-blocking verdict.
Suggestion; consistent with the sibling probe precedent and inherent to a cloud-only matcher measurement job (MEMORY: matcher semantics only provable in the real cloud action). Below threshold.
Suggestion; inherited empirically-validated assumption shared with the sibling probe job, fails toward UNATTEMPTED (safe) not false PERMITTED. Not introduced by this diff.

fix_decisions.claim_text: degenerate valid parse yields all-UNATTEMPTED with no warning
fix_decisions.evidence: Suggestion; fails toward UNATTEMPTED (safe direction), mitigated by the raw-denial-entries line. Parked.

phase3_findings.description: sibling-job ordinal inaccurate: DEVFLOW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md 'A third sibling job' vs implement-skill.md/matcher-probe.yml 'A second sibling'; matcher-probe.yml has 5 jobs. Loop elevates to blocking (contradicts HEAD + internal contradiction across 3 PR-added sites).

cross-doc ordinal inconsistency for implement-probe (three PR-added sites, two ordinals, neither matches the 5-job count) + job-intro states #401 anchor-denial as settled implement-tier fact vs 'suspected' in changeset/Shape-1 + run.sh 'byte-token identity' imprecise.
issues:read 'safe floor' framing undersells that omitting it (defaulting to none) is strictly tighter for reads; writes fail closed either way.
EXTRAS unpinned mirror + DENIED marker-only detection (both known/inherited).
